user_query:
Read the input files under input/ and produce four outputs under output/ as described below.

Inputs:
- input/raw_texts.jsonl: Each line is a JSON object with { id: string, text: string } containing mixed notes with potential secrets.
- input/memo_instructions.yaml: For each id in raw_texts.jsonl, provides the target kind (one of fact|decision|doc|note), tags (array of strings), and ttl_ms (number; 0 means no expiry).
- input/seed_store.jsonl: Existing memory items to be merged; some may be expired relative to the provided now timestamp.
- input/now.txt: A single ISO 8601 timestamp to use as "now" for createdAt and TTL calculations.

Tasks:
1) Redact secrets in each text using robust secret patterns (API keys like sk-..., bearer tokens, JWTs, PEM blocks, AWS AKIA keys, db connection strings like postgres:// or mysql://, GitHub PATs, etc.). For every input line, write a corresponding line to output/redacted.jsonl with fields: { id, redactedText, hadSecrets, matches }, where matches is an array of { rule, count }. Ensure no original secret substrings remain in redactedText; replace detections with safe placeholders (e.g., [REDACTED:TYPE]).

2) Build output/memory_store.jsonl by starting from input/seed_store.jsonl, purging items whose expiresAt is strictly before the timestamp in input/now.txt, then appending new MemoryItem entries for each id from input/raw_texts.jsonl using the following:
- id: same id from input
- kind: from input/memo_instructions.yaml (must be one of fact|decision|doc|note)
- text: the redactedText produced in step 1
- createdAt: exactly the timestamp from input/now.txt
- expiresAt: if ttl_ms > 0, compute now + ttl_ms and emit as ISO 8601 (UTC, with trailing Z); omit the field if ttl_ms == 0
- tags: exactly the tags array from memo_instructions.yaml
Other fields (source, meta) may be omitted.

3) Write output/purge_report.json summarizing the purge operation with fields: { expired_purged: number, purged_ids: string[], kept_seed_ids: string[] }.

4) Write output/index.json with summary stats: { total_new_items: number, by_kind: Record<string, number>, hadSecrets_count: number }. by_kind must count only the newly added items by their kind.

Important constraints:
- Do not leak any original secret substrings into any file under output/.
- Use the exact ISO timestamp from input/now.txt for createdAt of new items.
- TTL calculations must be deterministic using ttl_ms from memo_instructions.yaml and the exact now from input/now.txt.
- Write all outputs to the specified relative paths under output/.
